name: Johnny Fuller
description:
'''Johnny Fuller''' (April 20, 1929 – May 20, 1985) was an American [[West Coast blues|West Coast]] and [[electric blues]] singer and guitarist. Fuller showed musical diversity, performing in several musical genres including rhythm and blues, [[gospel music|gospel]] and [[rock and roll]].  His distinctive singing and guitar playing appeared on a number of 1950s [[San Francisco Bay Area]] recordings, although he ceased performing regularly by the late 1970s.

His best known recording, "Haunted House", was later [[cover version|covered]] with some success by [[Jumpin' Gene Simmons]]. His other better known tracks were "Crying Won't Make Me Stay", "All Night Long", "You Got Me Whistling" and "Johnny Ace's Last Letter."
